ground.note: Dark home base. Flips to oklch(95.5% 0.01 255) cool paper-grey in light theme.

surface-raised.note: Navy-tinted white film on dark; opaque white on light.

surface-lifted.note: Nested / hover / active. Faint cool-grey wash on light.

surface-sidebar.note: The sidebar's second neutral layer. oklch(93.5% 0.012 255) on light.

hairline.note: The one border. White-alpha on dark, Ink-Navy-alpha (rgb 15 23 43 / 0.12) on light.

ink-primary.note: Headings and any number a contractor acts on. Flips to oklch(21% 0.04 265) on light.

ink-secondary.note: Body copy. Deep Ink Navy (oklch(37% 0.044 257)) on light.

ink-muted.note: The contrast floor. ~7.6:1 on dark ground, ~4.7:1 on light. Nothing dimmer carries text.

on-accent.note: Constant dark ink on bright accents (cyan/amber/emerald buttons). Never flips.

instrument.note: The readout colour. Identical in both themes.

instrument-fg.note: Readable cyan text on a cyan tint. Pale in dark, deep (oklch(44% 0.125 224), ~5:1 on a cyan tint) in light.

signal-blue.note: 3D viewer overlays and input focus. Does not flip.

confirm.note: Terminal success only. -fg text pale (oklch(90% 0.11 165)) in dark, deep in light.

caution.note: The one warm signal. Attention/caution only — never a measurement, error, or decoration.

danger.note: Errors and destructive actions. -fg pale rose in dark, deep red in light.

info.note: Neutral informational states and non-measurement status badges.

paper.note: The (report) route. Pinned to light; never flips with app theme.

display.purpose: Page and workspace titles. One per screen.

title.purpose: Panel headers, control labels, column heads. Weight, not size, over body.

body.purpose: The workhorse reading size. 65–75ch max for prose.

label.purpose: Metadata, units, badges. The only place uppercase is allowed.

shadows.name: none
shadows.purpose: Default. The system is flat; depth is tonal. Prohibited on any translucent surface.

shadows.name: paper-document
shadows.purpose: Only the opaque proposal document earns a paper-on-a-desk shadow.

motion.name: duration-state
motion.purpose: Hover / focus / theme feedback. Users are in flow; no choreography.

breakpoints.name: lg
breakpoints.note: Sidebar becomes a fixed 260px column at lg.

components.name: Primary Button (Hero)
components.description: The one action per surface that advances the job. Brightest object on screen; constant dark ink in both themes.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: oklch(78.9% 0.154 211.53); color: oklch(13% 0.05 264); border: none; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 20px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(86.5% 0.127 207.078); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(78.9% 0.154 211.53); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Quiet Button
components.description: Recessive secondary action. Cyan tint, cyan-foreground text, so the solid hero stays the brightest object.
components.css: .ds-btn-quiet { background: color-mix(in oklab, oklch(78.9% 0.154 211.53) 10%, transparent); color: oklch(89% 0.09 205); border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, oklch(86.5% 0.127 207.078) 30%, transparent); border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 16px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 20px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-quiet:hover { background: color-mix(in oklab, oklch(78.9% 0.154 211.53) 20%, transparent); }

components.name: Theme Toggle
components.description: Icon button in the dashboard header. Sun in dark (click for light), moon in light. Writes data-theme to <html> and persists to localStorage; an inline script replays it before paint. No stored choice follows the OS.
components.html: <button class="ds-theme-toggle" aria-label="Switch to light mode"><svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="4"/><path d="M12 2v2M12 20v2M2 12h2M20 12h2M4.9 4.9l1.4 1.4M17.7 17.7l1.4 1.4M19.1 4.9l-1.4 1.4M6.3 17.7l-1.4 1.4"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-theme-toggle { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 36px; width: 36px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid rgb(216 227 255 / 0.14); background: rgb(216 227 255 / 0.07); color: oklch(87% 0.028 262); cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms, color 150ms; } .ds-theme-toggle:hover { background: rgb(216 227 255 / 0.13); color: #fff; } .ds-theme-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(78.9% 0.154 211.53); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Panel
components.description: The workhorse container. Surface Raised fill, one hairline border, no shadow.
components.html:
Roof summary

2,140 sq ft across 6 roof faces.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: rgb(216 227 255 / 0.07); border: 1px solid rgb(216 227 255 / 0.14); border-radius: 16px; padding: 24px; } .ds-panel-title { margin: 0 0 8px; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; color: #fff; } .ds-panel-body { margin: 0; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px; color: oklch(87% 0.028 262); }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Read-only status carrier from the quartet. Shown here as caution/amber; swap the base + -fg token for confirm, danger, or info.
components.css: .ds-status-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 4px 10px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.025em; background: color-mix(in oklab, oklch(80% 0.15 78) 10%, transparent); border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, oklch(80% 0.15 78) 25%, transparent); color: oklch(90% 0.08 88); }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Surface Raised fill, hairline border, signal-blue focus. Error state shifts the border to danger with a message below.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; background: rgb(216 227 255 / 0.07); border: 1px solid rgb(216 227 255 / 0.14); border-radius: 12px; padding: 12px 16px; font-size: 14px; color: #fff; outline: none; transition: border-color 150ms; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(70.4% 0.045 258); } .ds-input:focus { border-color: oklch(62.3% 0.214 259.815); } .ds-input[aria-invalid="true"] { border-color: oklch(63.7% 0.234 25.3); }

components.name: Nav Item
components.description: Sidebar row on the surface-sidebar layer. Ink Secondary at rest, Ink Primary + Surface Lifted fill when active. No accent stripe.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: block; border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px 16px; font-size: 14px; color: oklch(87% 0.028 262); text-decoration: none; transition: background 150ms, color 150ms; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: rgb(216 227 255 / 0.07); color: #fff; } .ds-nav-item--active { background: rgb(216 227 255 / 0.13); color: #fff; }

narrative.overview: Flat, layered by tone, never by shadow. Its home base is a deep Ink Navy ground carrying navy-tinted translucent panels, separated by a single hairline rule. But 'legible in any light' is now literal: a light theme — cool paper-grey ground, white panels, dark ink — is a first-class daylight mode, because a contractor reads this on a bright roof as often as at a desk. Dark is the identity and default; light is the same notebook held up to the sun. Instrument Cyan is the one thing that never moves between them: the readout colour is identical in both themes. Around that fixed cyan, one deliberate warm note — Amber — is held in strict reserve for attention and caution, never a measurement and never an error.
keyCharacteristics: Flat by doctrine — depth from tonal layering, never shadow
Two themes, one hero: Instrument Cyan is constant; the ground, ink, and surfaces flip
One hairline rule does all structural separation (white-alpha in dark, ink-alpha in light)
Small, dense, workmanlike type (14px body) — but never at the cost of contrast
Cyan for measurement truth; Amber, in reserve, for attention only
Soft radii (12–16px) to keep a technical product feeling unhurried

rules.name: The Readout Rule
rules.body: Instrument Cyan means 'a measurement or the action that produces one,' and it is identical in both themes. Never a brand flourish, link colour, or decoration.

rules.name: The One Warm Note Rule
rules.body: Amber is the system's only warm colour, and it means attention/caution and nothing else — never a measurement, error, success, or decoration.

rules.name: The Constant-On-Accent Rule
rules.body: Text on a bright accent uses on-accent (constant dark), never ground. ground flips near-white in light mode; a button labelled in ground would vanish on its own cyan.

rules.name: The Ink Floor Rule
rules.body: Ink Muted is the dimmest colour permitted to carry text, measured against whichever ground is active. Route every text colour through the ink-* tokens; the codebase is clean of raw slate-500.

rules.name: The One Rule Rule
rules.body: All structural separation uses the hairline at one weight. No 2px borders, no colored stripes, no accent bars. More emphasis than a hairline means spacing or tone.

rules.name: The Weight-Not-Size Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy between a label and its value is carried by weight and colour, not size. The one exception is a single hero number that may size up in cyan to lead its panel.

rules.name: The Uppercase Confinement Rule
rules.body: Uppercase is a 12px label treatment only. A measurement, heading, or sentence is never uppercased.

rules.name: The Two-Layer Rule
rules.body: Ground → raised → lifted is the entire elevation vocabulary, in either theme. A third layer means the layout is too nested.

rules.name: The Dark-Instrument Rule
rules.body: The live 3D viewers stay dark in both themes via a scoped .surface-dark block. The model renders on a dark canvas; a light control bar over it would strand its own tool colours.

rules.name: The Scrim Rule
rules.body: Blur is permitted only where unpredictable content sits underneath, always paired with an opaque scrim. On the ground, blur is prohibited.

rules.name: The Anti-Pattern Test
rules.body: If a panel appears to float above the page rather than sit on it, the treatment is wrong. Surfaces rest; they do not hover.

dos: Do use Instrument Cyan only for measurement truth and the primary action — and keep it identical in both themes.
Do reserve Amber for attention and caution alone.
Do label bright-accent buttons with on-accent, never ground.
Do route every colour through a semantic token so a theme swap needs no per-component work.
Do express every border as the 1px hairline at one weight.
Do build depth from the two-layer tonal scale and nothing else.
Do keep body copy at 14px / Ink Secondary and treat Ink Muted as the dimmest text — in either theme.
Do keep the live 3D viewers on .surface-dark so their tool colours stay legible.
Do keep the (report) route on its light paper tokens.
Do render a visible focus ring on every interactive element (WCAG 2.2 AA).
donts: Don't surface CAD and engineering complexity — no exposed parameter panels or tolerance sliders in front of a contractor.
Don't put pipeline vocabulary on screen: facet, normal, mesh, RANSAC, tolerance, reconstruction.
Don't let Amber mean anything but caution.
Don't put text-ground on a bright accent; it disappears in light mode. Use on-accent.
Don't hardcode a raw palette utility on an app surface — it won't flip. Tokens only (the 3D viewers and paper report are the two exceptions).
Don't add a shadow to anything on the translucent ground.
Don't use backdrop-blur on the ground; only over the viewer canvas or a sticky bar, with an opaque scrim.
Don't signal an error, warning, or state with colour alone.
Don't uppercase anything larger than a 12px label.
Don't use a colored left-border stripe or a border heavier than 1px to emphasize a panel.
Don't let Confirm Green mean 'in progress.' It means done, and only done.
Don't introduce a webfont without a deliberate reason.
